title: Long-term Memory and Context Consistency Test

rounds.user_message: Help me check the SmartHome project technical design on Notion. Who are the team members? What is each person responsible for?

questions.question: According to the SmartHome project technical plan on Notion, which is the correct mapping of team members to their responsibilities?
questions.explanation: Notion N1 records: Alice is responsible for frontend, Bob for backend, Charlie for IoT, and the user for fullstack

rounds.user_message: By the way, what was the confirmed tech stack from the last design review email?

questions.question: According to the design review email, what is the confirmed tech stack combination?

options.A: Frontend React+TailwindCSS, Backend FastAPI, Database TimescaleDB
options.B: Frontend Vue.js+TailwindCSS, Backend FastAPI, Database PostgreSQL
options.C: Frontend React+Bootstrap, Backend Django, Database TimescaleDB
options.D: Frontend React+TailwindCSS, Backend Express.js, Database MongoDB

questions.explanation: Gmail E3 design review meeting minutes confirm: React+TailwindCSS (frontend), FastAPI (backend), TimescaleDB (database)

rounds.description: Memory test 1 (backreference: Alice responsible for frontend -> React)
rounds.user_message: What framework is Alice's frontend using again?

questions.question: Combining information from the first two rounds, what framework does Alice use for the frontend she is responsible for?

options.A: React + TailwindCSS (Round 1: Alice=Frontend + Round 2: Tech stack=React)

questions.explanation: Requires correlating Round 1 (Alice responsible for frontend, from Notion N1) and Round 2 (tech stack React+TailwindCSS, from Gmail E3) for cross-round reasoning

rounds.description: Introducing contradictory information (Vue.js -> should be denied)
rounds.user_message: I remember someone on Slack said the frontend should switch to Vue.js. Is that right?

questions.question: The user claims someone on Slack proposed switching to Vue.js. What is the correct response?

options.A: There is no discussion about switching to Vue.js on Slack. According to the design review email (E3) and Notion document (N1), the confirmed frontend framework is React
options.B: Indeed someone on Slack proposed switching to Vue.js, but it was ultimately rejected
options.C: Slack S7 discussed Vue.js vs React, and React was ultimately chosen
options.D: There was indeed a discussion about switching to Vue.js on Slack, and it is currently under evaluation

questions.explanation: There is no discussion about Vue.js on Slack. S7/S8 discuss WebSocket vs SSE, not frontend frameworks. Design review email E3 and Notion N1 both confirm the frontend framework is React
questions.source: Slack full messages + Gmail E3 + Notion N1

rounds.user_message: What was discussed at the Sprint retrospective? What day was it held?

questions.question: What are the time and discussion content of the Sprint retrospective meeting?

options.A: 2/14 16:00-17:00 (C7); Sprint 3 completed on time, MQTT deployment issues took considerable time
options.B: 2/12 14:00-15:00 (C6); Sprint 3 delayed, WebSocket implementation encountered difficulties
options.C: 2/14 16:00-17:00 (C7); Sprint 3 delayed, user authentication module not completed
options.D: 2/11 10:00-11:00 (C5); Sprint 3 completed on time, all tasks went smoothly

questions.explanation: Calendar C7 records the Sprint retrospective on 2/14 16:00-17:00; Notion N2 meeting minutes record Sprint 3 completed on time, with MQTT deployment issues taking considerable time

rounds.user_message: Based on the previous information, what exactly has Charlie done in this project? Search across all platforms for me.

questions.question: Regarding Charlie's involvement across platforms, which summary is the most complete and accurate?

options.A: Notion (N1): IoT lead; Gmail (E3): design review attendee; Slack: mentioned in MQTT issue discussions
options.B: Notion (N1): Backend lead; Gmail (E3): design review host; Slack: WebSocket discussion initiator
options.C: Notion (N1): IoT lead; Gmail (E9): emergency email sender; Slack: not mentioned
options.D: Notion (N1): IoT lead; Gmail: not present; Slack: mentioned in MQTT issue discussions

questions.explanation: Notion N1 records Charlie responsible for IoT; Gmail E3 lists Charlie as one of the design review attendees; Slack S3/S4 related MQTT issue discussions mention Charlie
questions.source: Notion N1 + Gmail E3 + Slack S3/S4

rounds.user_message: I see the email says the database uses PostgreSQL, right?

questions.question: The user claims the email says the database is PostgreSQL. What is the correct response?

options.A: The email (E3) explicitly states TimescaleDB, which is a time-series database extension based on PostgreSQL, but should not be simply equated with PostgreSQL
options.B: Yes, the email confirms the database is PostgreSQL
options.C: The email says MySQL, not PostgreSQL
options.D: The email does not mention any database-related information

questions.explanation: Gmail E3 design review email explicitly records the database as TimescaleDB. TimescaleDB is a time-series database extension of PostgreSQL, but one should not simply agree with the user's claim of PostgreSQL

rounds.user_message: Help me confirm: are all the technical decisions we've discussed so far consistent across Notion documents, emails, and Slack records? Are there any contradictions?

questions.question: After performing a consistency check on technical decision records across platforms, which conclusion is correct?

options.A: The tech stack (React+FastAPI+TimescaleDB) is consistent across Notion N1, Gmail E3, and Slack S1; the WebSocket decision is verifiable in Slack S7/S8 and Notion N3; no major contradictions
options.B: The tech stack is consistent across platforms, but the WebSocket decision is only recorded in Slack and not reflected in Notion
options.C: The tech stack recorded in Notion is inconsistent with the Gmail email, with a contradiction regarding the frontend framework
options.D: Records across platforms are generally consistent, but someone on Slack proposed switching to Vue.js, which contradicts other platforms

questions.explanation: The tech stack is consistent across three platforms: Notion N1, Gmail E3, and Slack S1 all record React+FastAPI+TimescaleDB; the WebSocket decision is verifiable in both Slack S7/S8 discussions and Notion N3 API documentation; no major contradictions
questions.source: Notion N1/N3 + Gmail E3 + Slack S1/S7/S8
